headline: Public Wi-Fi Audit Finds 72% of Hotspots Vulnerable
context: NACSA audit of 4,500 public Wi-Fi hotspots found 72% had security vulnerabilities exposing users to data theft.

cards.big: 72% of 4,500 public Wi-Fi hotspots vulnerable to data theft
cards.sub: Government buildings malls and transport hubs among insecure locations

cards.big: 28% of government free Wi-Fi used outdated WPA2 encryption
cards.sub: Attackers could intercept login credentials from connected users

cards.big: MCMC free Wi-Fi covers 8,200 hotspots but security was optional
cards.sub: New mandatory requirements carry compliance deadline of December 2026

cards.big: 2.8 million Malaysians use public Wi-Fi as primary internet access
cards.sub: B40 users who cannot afford home broadband disproportionately exposed

cards.big: Wi-Fi security tests whether free internet comes with adequate protection for users
72% of public Wi-Fi hotspots vulnerable. Free connectivity without security exposes users to greater risk.
